   150→
   151→### Pattern 1: D2LTrainConfig as Pydantic BaseModel
   152→**What:** All training hyperparameters in a validated, serializable config object
   153→**When to use:** Always — enables checkpoint JSON, MLflow param logging, argparse integration
   154→
   155→```python
   156→# Source: CONTEXT.md decision + pydantic 2.x docs
   157→from __future__ import annotations
   158→from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
   159→from pathlib import Path
   160→
   161→class D2LTrainConfig(BaseModel):
   162→    base_model_name: str = "Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-Next"
   163→    sakana_checkpoint_path: str
   164→    num_steps: int = 100
   165→    lr: float = 2e-4
   166→    alpha: float = 0.5          # KL/CE blend weight
   167→    temperature: float = 2.0
   168→    checkpoint_every: int = 100
   169→    full_checkpoint_every: int = 500
   170→    checkpoint_dir: str = "./checkpoints"
   171→    experiment_name: str = "d2l-qwen3"
   172→    dry_run: bool = False
   173→    smoke_test: bool = False
   174→    dataset_path: str | None = None
   175→    grad_clip: float = 1.0
   176→    warmup_steps: int = 10
   177→    lora_r: int = 8
   178→    max_length: int = 512
   179→```
   180→
   181→### Pattern 2: Two-Pass Teacher/Student Forward
   182→**What:** Separate forward passes to prevent answer token leakage into activation extraction
   183→**When to use:** Every training step — this is the core TRAIN-02 requirement
   184→
   185→```python
   186→# Source: REQUIREMENTS.md TRAIN-02 + d2l_probe.py extract_activations_with_model()
   187→import torch
   188→
   189→def _training_step(
   190→    record: dict[str, str],
   191→    base_model: Any,
   192→    tokenizer: Any,
   193→    hypernet: Any,
   194→    hc: Any,
   195→    config: D2LTrainConfig,
   196→) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, dict[str, float]]:
   197→    # Pass 1: activation extraction from context-only text (NO answer tokens)
   198→    features, attn_mask = extract_activations_with_model(
   199→        text=record["activation_text"],
   200→        model=base_model,
   201→        tokenizer=tokenizer,
   202→        layer_indices=list(hc.layer_indices),
   203→        max_length=config.max_length,
   204→    )
   205→
   206→    # Hypernetwork forward: features → LoRA A/B tensors (live graph nodes)
   207→    lora_dict, _ = hypernet.generate_weights(features, attn_mask, None)
   208→
   209→    # Pass 2: teacher logits from full text (context + answer)
   210→    teacher_inputs = tokenizer(
   211→        record["teacher_text"],
   212→        return_tensors="pt",
   213→        truncation=True,
   214→        max_length=config.max_length,
   215→    )
   216→    teacher_inputs = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in teacher_inputs.items()}
   217→
   218→    with torch.no_grad():
   219→        teacher_out = base_model(**teacher_inputs)
   220→    teacher_logits = teacher_out.logits  # (1, seq_len, vocab)
   221→
   222→    # Student pass: same full text, but with LoRA-patched model
   223→    act_tokens = tokenizer(
   224→        record["activation_text"],
   225→        truncation=True,
   226→        max_length=config.max_length,
   227→    )
   228→    answer_start = len(act_tokens["input_ids"])
   229→
   230→    with apply_functional_lora(base_model, lora_dict, hc):
   231→        student_out = base_model(**teacher_inputs)
   232→    student_logits = student_out.logits  # (1, seq_len, vocab)
   233→
   234→    loss, metrics = _compute_kl_ce_loss(
   235→        student_logits, teacher_logits, answer_start, config
   236→    )
   237→    return loss, metrics
   238→```
   239→
   240→### Pattern 3: KL+CE Blended Loss with Answer Span Masking
   241→**What:** Pure-tensor loss computation — testable without a real model
   242→**When to use:** Every training step; also standalone in unit tests
   243→
   244→```python
   245→# Source: verified locally with torch 2.10.0
   246→import torch.nn.functional as F
   247→
   248→def _compute_kl_ce_loss(
   249→    student_logits: torch.Tensor,   # (1, seq_len, vocab)
   250→    teacher_logits: torch.Tensor,   # (1, seq_len, vocab)
   251→    answer_start: int,
   252→    config: D2LTrainConfig,
   253→) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, dict[str, float]]:
   254→    """Compute blended KL+CE loss on answer span only.
   255→
   256→    KL returns ~0 when student_logits == teacher_logits (verified).
   257→    """
   258→    T = config.temperature
   259→    alpha = config.alpha
   260→
   261→    # Slice to answer span only
   262→    s_ans = student_logits[:, answer_start:, :]   # (1, ans_len, vocab)
   263→    t_ans = teacher_logits[:, answer_start:, :]   # (1, ans_len, vocab)
   264→
   265→    # KL divergence: kl_div expects log-probs for input, probs for target
   266→    s_log = F.log_softmax(s_ans / T, dim=-1)
   267→    t_prob = F.softmax(t_ans / T, dim=-1)
   268→    kl_loss = F.kl_div(s_log, t_prob, reduction="batchmean")
   269→
   270→    # CE loss: teacher hard labels
   271→    vocab = s_ans.shape[-1]
   272→    t_labels = t_ans.argmax(dim=-1)   # (1, ans_len)
   273→    ce_loss = F.cross_entropy(
   274→        s_ans.reshape(-1, vocab),
   275→        t_labels.reshape(-1),
   276→    )
   277→
   278→    total_loss = alpha * kl_loss + (1.0 - alpha) * ce_loss
   279→    return total_loss, {
   280→        "kl_loss": kl_loss.item(),
   281→        "ce_loss": ce_loss.item(),
   282→        "total_loss": total_loss.item(),
   283→    }
   284→```
   285→
   286→### Pattern 4: Cosine LR with Linear Warmup (SequentialLR)
   287→**What:** Native PyTorch scheduler chain — no additional imports
   288→**When to use:** Always; warmup prevents early divergence
   289→
   290→```python
   291→# Source: verified with torch.optim.lr_scheduler, torch 2.10.0
   292→# CRITICAL: optimizer.step() BEFORE scheduler.step() (PyTorch 1.1+ requirement)
   293→
   294→trainable_params = [p for p in hypernet.parameters() if p.requires_grad]
   295→optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(trainable_params, lr=config.lr)
   296→
   297→warmup_sched = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(
   298→    optimizer,
   299→    start_factor=0.01,
   300→    end_factor=1.0,
   301→    total_iters=config.warmup_steps,
   302→)
   303→cosine_sched = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(
   304→    optimizer,
   305→    T_max=max(1, config.num_steps - config.warmup_steps),
   306→    eta_min=1e-6,
   307→)
   308→scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.SequentialLR(
   309→    optimizer,
   310→    schedulers=[warmup_sched, cosine_sched],
   311→    milestones=[config.warmup_steps],
   312→)
   313→
   314→# In training loop:
   315→loss.backward()
   316→grad_norm = torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(trainable_params, config.grad_clip)
   317→optimizer.step()
   318→scheduler.step()          # AFTER optimizer.step()
   319→optimizer.zero_grad()
   320→```
   321→
   322→### Pattern 5: Tiered Checkpoint Saving
   323→**What:** Frequent lightweight saves (hypernet only) + periodic full saves (optimizer + RNG)
   324→**When to use:** Every N steps per config.checkpoint_every / config.full_checkpoint_every
   325→
   326→```python
   327→# Source: CONTEXT.md decisions + torch.save verified
   328→import torch, json
   329→from pathlib import Path
   330→
   331→def _save_checkpoint(
   332→    step: int, hypernet: Any, optimizer: Any, scheduler: Any,
   333→    config: D2LTrainConfig, hc: Any, best_loss: float, full: bool = False,
   334→) -> Path:
   335→    ckpt_dir = Path(config.checkpoint_dir)
   336→    ckpt_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   337→    path = ckpt_dir / f"ckpt-{step}.pt"
   338→
   339→    payload: dict[str, Any] = {
   340→        "step": step,
   341→        "hypernet_state_dict": hypernet.state_dict(),
   342→        "config_json": config.model_dump(),    # Pydantic → dict for JSON-safe storage
   343→        "attention_layer_indices": list(hc.layer_indices),
   344→        "best_loss": best_loss,
   345→    }
   346→    if full:
   347→        payload["optimizer_state_dict"] = optimizer.state_dict()
   348→        payload["scheduler_state_dict"] = scheduler.state_dict()
   349→        payload["rng_state"] = torch.get_rng_state()
   350→        if torch.cuda.is_available():
   351→            payload["cuda_rng_state"] = torch.cuda.get_rng_state()
   352→
   353→    torch.save(payload, path)
   354→    return path
   355→```

   385→### Pattern 7: Test Mock Strategy (no GPU required)
   386→**What:** sys.modules injection + SimpleNamespace fake models — matches established project pattern
   387→**When to use:** All 14 TEST-01 tests
   388→
   389→```python
   390→# Source: established pattern from test_d2l_lora.py and test_d2l_weight_transfer.py
   391→from types import SimpleNamespace
   392→import torch
   393→import torch.nn as nn
   394→
   395→# Fake hypernet with aggregator (frozen) + head (trainable)
   396→class _FakeHypernet(nn.Module):
   397→    def __init__(self, n_layers: int, r: int, d: int, vocab: int) -> None:
   398→        super().__init__()
   399→        self.aggregator = nn.Linear(d, d)   # frozen in tests
   400→        self.head = nn.Linear(d, vocab * r) # trainable
   401→
   402→    def generate_weights(
   403→        self, features: torch.Tensor, attn_mask: torch.Tensor, _: Any
   404→    ) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], None]:
   405→        # Return minimal lora_dict matching apply_functional_lora expectations
   406→        n_layers = features.shape[1]
   407→        lora_dict = {
   408→            "q_proj": {
   409→                "A": torch.randn(1, n_layers, r, d, requires_grad=True),
   410→                "B": torch.randn(1, n_layers, r, d, requires_grad=True),
   411→            }
   412→        }
   413→        return lora_dict, None
   414→
   415→# Freeze aggregator to match training invariant
   416→for name, p in fake_hypernet.named_parameters():
   417→    if name.startswith("aggregator"):
   418→        p.requires_grad_(False)
   419→```
   420→
   421→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   422→- **Calling scheduler.step() before optimizer.step():** PyTorch 1.1+ skips the first LR value. Always: backward → clip → optimizer.step() → scheduler.step() → zero_grad.
   423→- **Using PEFT get_peft_model during training:** Severs autograd graph. Use apply_functional_lora() (Phase 28) exclusively.
   424→- **Computing KL on full sequence (not answer span):** Leaks context token distributions into loss signal. Always slice [:, answer_start:, :] before loss.
   425→- **Using hypernet.generate_weights() with torch.no_grad():** The hypernetwork forward must remain in the autograd graph for gradients to flow back through lora_a and lora_b. Only teacher_out forward uses no_grad.
   426→- **Calling hypernet.eval() during training:** eval() disables dropout but more importantly signals inference mode. Keep hypernet in train() mode; base_model stays in eval() mode.
   427→- **Including answer tokens in activation_text pass:** The activation extraction pass must use activation_text (context only). Using teacher_text here leaks the answer into the hypernetwork's conditioning — the exact failure mode this pipeline is designed to prevent.
   428→
   429→---
   430→
   431→## Don't Hand-Roll
   432→
   433→| Problem | Don't Build | Use Instead | Why |
   434→|---------|-------------|-------------|-----|
   435→| KL divergence | Custom kl formula | `F.kl_div(log_softmax, softmax, reduction='batchmean')` | Numerically stable; verified ~0 when equal |
   436→| Warmup + cosine LR | Manual step counting | `SequentialLR([LinearLR, CosineAnnealingLR], milestones=[warmup_steps])` | Native; correct first-step behavior |
   437→| Gradient clipping | Manual norm computation | `torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(params, max_norm)` | Returns actual norm for logging; handles edge cases |
   438→| Experiment tracking | Custom metric logger | `mlflow.log_metrics(dict, step=step)` | Already installed; supports local + remote; no code changes for team server |
   439→| Config serialization | Custom JSON dump | `config.model_dump()` from Pydantic BaseModel | Type-safe, validates on construction, round-trips through JSON |
   440→| Checkpoint tensor storage | Custom format | `torch.save(dict, path)` / `torch.load(path, weights_only=False)` | Established pattern from sakana_d2l.py; weights_only=False needed for Python objects |
   441→
   442→**Key insight:** The training loop has no novel algorithmic problems — every sub-problem (optimizer, scheduler, loss, checkpointing, tracking) has a battle-tested library solution already installed. The implementation work is assembly and wiring, not invention.
   443→
   444→---
   445→
   446→## Common Pitfalls
   447→
   448→### Pitfall 1: KL Loss Sign / Reduction Mode
   449→**What goes wrong:** `F.kl_div` with `reduction='sum'` gives unbounded values proportional to sequence length; with `reduction='mean'` averages over all elements including the distribution dimension.
   450→**Why it happens:** `reduction='batchmean'` is the mathematically correct reduction (sum / batch_size), but it's not the default.
   451→**How to avoid:** Always use `reduction='batchmean'`. Verify with identical logits → near-zero loss.
   452→**Warning signs:** KL loss > 10 on first step with random init (should be ~2-5 with temperature scaling).
   453→
   454→### Pitfall 2: Hypernetwork generate_weights() Inside torch.no_grad()
   455→**What goes wrong:** If hypernet.generate_weights() is called inside a `torch.no_grad()` block, lora_a/lora_b tensors become non-leaf detached tensors with no grad_fn. Loss backward produces None grads on head parameters.
   456→**Why it happens:** The teacher forward correctly uses no_grad (teacher is not updated), and developers copy this pattern to the hypernet forward.
   457→**How to avoid:** Only the teacher base model forward uses `torch.no_grad()`. Hypernet forward and student base model forward (inside apply_functional_lora context) must be outside no_grad.
   458→**Warning signs:** `p.grad is None` for all head.* params after backward even though requires_grad=True.
   459→
   460→### Pitfall 3: answer_start Boundary Off-by-One
   461→**What goes wrong:** Tokenizers may add BOS tokens, trailing spaces, or different subword splits between activation_text and teacher_text, causing answer_start to be off by 1-2 tokens.
   462→**Why it happens:** String prefix match assumes tokenization of activation_text produces the exact prefix of tokenized teacher_text. This is not always true across tokenizers.
   463→**How to avoid:** Tokenize both separately, use `len(tokenizer(activation_text)["input_ids"])` as answer_start. Verify that teacher_tokens[:answer_start] approximately matches activation_tokens. Add a DEBUG-level log of the boundary for smoke-test runs.
   464→**Warning signs:** CE loss that is exactly cross-entropy-of-random (log(vocab_size) ≈ 11.9) regardless of training, suggesting answer span is empty or contains only context tokens.
   465→
   466→### Pitfall 4: Checkpoint Readability with Python Objects
   467→**What goes wrong:** `torch.load(path, weights_only=True)` raises when the checkpoint contains non-tensor objects (D2LTrainConfig dict, HypernetConfig, step count integer).
   468→**Why it happens:** PyTorch defaults changed toward weights_only=True for security. Checkpoints mixing tensors and Python objects require weights_only=False.
   469→**How to avoid:** Always use `weights_only=False` for d2l training checkpoints. This matches the established pattern in sakana_d2l.py. Document this in the checkpoint-loading helper.
   470→**Warning signs:** `_pickle.UnpicklingError` or a PyTorch FutureWarning on load.
   471→
   472→### Pitfall 5: Optimizer Scope Includes Frozen Params
   473→**What goes wrong:** `torch.optim.AdamW(hypernet.parameters(), lr=lr)` includes frozen aggregator parameters. Even with requires_grad=False, this can cause confusion and wastes memory in optimizer state.
   474→**Why it happens:** Default convenience of passing all parameters.
   475→**How to avoid:** Always filter: `trainable = [p for p in hypernet.parameters() if p.requires_grad]`. Verified locally: aggregator.* params have None gradient after backward when excluded from optimizer scope.
   476→**Warning signs:** Optimizer state_dict is larger than expected (includes Adam moment buffers for frozen params).
   477→
   478→### Pitfall 6: base_model in train() vs eval() Mode
   479→**What goes wrong:** If base_model is in train() mode during teacher/student passes, dropout (if any) introduces randomness into teacher logits, making the distillation target non-deterministic within a step.
   480→**Why it happens:** Default model state after from_pretrained is eval() but it's easy to accidentally call train().
   481→**How to avoid:** Keep base_model.eval() throughout the training loop. Only hypernet is in train() mode. This also disables batch norm updates in the base model.
   482→**Warning signs:** CE loss has high variance between runs with identical data and seed.
   483→
   484→### Pitfall 7: Dry-Run Requires Real Model (GPU)
   485→**What goes wrong:** Attempting to run `--dry-run` on CPU with real Qwen3-Coder-Next fails with OOM or extremely slow forward pass (48-layer model).
   486→**Why it happens:** CONTEXT.md explicitly states dry-run requires GPU. This is intentional.
   487→**How to avoid:** Document clearly in the argparse help text. The smoke-test and unit tests use needle_dataset + fake models and work on CPU.
   488→**Warning signs:** Dry-run taking >30 seconds on CPU (success criteria requires <30 seconds — only achievable on GPU with real model).
   489→
   490→---
   491→
   492→## Code Examples
   493→
   494→### Two-Pass Loss Computation (Verified)
   495→```python
   496→# Source: verified locally with torch 2.10.0
   497→import torch, torch.nn.functional as F
   498→
   499→def _compute_kl_ce_loss(
   500→    student_logits: torch.Tensor,  # (1, full_seq, vocab)
   501→    teacher_logits: torch.Tensor,  # (1, full_seq, vocab)
   502→    answer_start: int,
   503→    alpha: float = 0.5,
   504→    temperature: float = 2.0,
   505→) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, float, float]:
   506→    T = temperature
   507→    s = student_logits[:, answer_start:, :]  # answer span only
   508→    t = teacher_logits[:, answer_start:, :]
   509→
   510→    # KL: F.kl_div(log_softmax, softmax, reduction='batchmean')
   511→    kl = F.kl_div(F.log_softmax(s / T, dim=-1), F.softmax(t / T, dim=-1), reduction="batchmean")
   512→
   513→    # CE: argmax teacher labels
   514→    ce = F.cross_entropy(s.reshape(-1, s.shape[-1]), t.argmax(-1).reshape(-1))
   515→
   516→    total = alpha * kl + (1 - alpha) * ce
   517→    return total, kl.item(), ce.item()
   518→
   519→# TEST-01 unit test: KL = 0 when student_logits == teacher_logits
   520→logits = torch.randn(1, 5, 100)  # same for both
   521→_, kl, _ = _compute_kl_ce_loss(logits, logits, answer_start=2)
   522→assert abs(kl) < 1e-5, f"KL should be ~0 when equal, got {kl}"
   523→```

   525→### Smoke-Test Loop Pattern
   526→```python
   527→# Source: CONTEXT.md + REQUIREMENTS.md TRAIN-07
   528→records = generate_needle_dataset(n=10)  # deterministic
   529→losses: list[float] = []
   530→
   531→for step, record in enumerate(records[:config.num_steps]):
   532→    loss, metrics = _training_step(record, base_model, tokenizer, hypernet, hc, config)
   533→
   534→    loss.backward()
   535→    grad_norm = torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(trainable_params, config.grad_clip)
   536→    optimizer.step()
   537→    scheduler.step()
   538→    optimizer.zero_grad()
   539→
   540→    assert loss.isfinite(), f"Non-finite loss at step {step}: {loss.item()}"
   541→    losses.append(metrics["total_loss"])
   542→
   543→# Verify decreasing trend (step 5 loss < step 1 loss)
   544→assert losses[-1] < losses[0], f"Loss did not decrease: {losses[0]:.4f} → {losses[-1]:.4f}"

   549→# Source: verified locally — aggregator params get None grad

   552→optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(trainable_params, lr=config.lr)
   553→
   554→# After backward — verified aggregator.* params have None grad:

   578→## State of the Art
   579→
   580→| Old Approach | Current Approach | When Changed | Impact |
   581→|--------------|------------------|--------------|--------|
   582→| PEFT get_peft_model for functional LoRA | F.linear closures via apply_functional_lora | Phase 28 | Autograd continuity preserved through hypernetwork |
   583→| warmup_ratio with transformers scheduler | SequentialLR(LinearLR, CosineAnnealingLR) | PyTorch 1.13+ | No transformers dependency in training loop scheduler |
   584→| Manual JSON config handling | Pydantic BaseModel with .model_dump() | Project decision | Type-safe, validates on construction, round-trips cleanly |
   585→
   586→**Deprecated/outdated:**
   587→- `torch.load(path)` without `weights_only=False`: FutureWarning in PyTorch 2.x when checkpoint contains non-tensor Python objects. Use `weights_only=False` explicitly (matches sakana_d2l.py pattern).

   590→
   591→## Open Questions
   592→
   593→1. **Hypernet generate_weights() signature: does it return (lora_dict, layernorm_dict) or just lora_dict?**
   594→   - What we know: `sakana_d2l.py` line 488 shows `lora_dict, layernorm_dict = hypernet.generate_weights(features, attn_mask, None)` — two return values
   595→   - What's unclear: Whether layernorm_dict is None or contains tensors; training step ignores it but should unpack correctly
   596→   - Recommendation: Always unpack as `lora_dict, _ = hypernet.generate_weights(...)` and log a warning if `_` is not None
   597→
   598→2. **Smoke-test decreasing loss guarantee with random init**
   599→   - What we know: With random init, loss may not decrease monotonically over 5 steps on synthetic needle data
   600→   - What's unclear: Whether 5 steps with lr=2e-4 is enough to observe a decreasing trend on synthetic data
   601→   - Recommendation: Use a small fixed random seed and verify on needle data before finalizing; consider `assert losses[-1] < losses[0]` with tolerance or check minimum over last 3 steps vs first step
   602→
   603→3. **mlflow in model-training pyproject.toml — [gpu] optional or hard dependency?**
   604→   - What we know: MLflow is already in root pyproject.toml (version >=3.8.1); CONTEXT.md says "MLflow as required dependency in model-training package"
   605→   - What's unclear: Whether it should go in `dependencies` or `[project.optional-dependencies] gpu`
   606→   - Recommendation: Add to `dependencies` (not optional) since it has no GPU requirement and the CONTEXT.md says "required dependency"

154→def extract_activations_with_model(
   155→    text: str,
   156→    model: Any,
   157→    tokenizer: Any,
   158→    layer_indices: list[int] | None = None,
   159→    model_name: str | None = None,
   160→    max_length: int = 512,
   161→) -> tuple[Any, Any]:
   162→    """Extract per-layer hidden state activations from a pre-loaded model.
   163→
   164→    Runs text through the model with output_hidden_states=True and stacks
   165→    activations from the specified layer indices. Uses hidden_states[i] directly
   166→    (no +1 offset) — consistent with existing sakana_d2l.py convention.
   167→
   168→    Args:
   169→        text: Input text to tokenize and process.
   170→        model: Pre-loaded nn.Module in eval mode.
   171→        tokenizer: Pre-loaded tokenizer.
   172→        layer_indices: Which hidden state indices to extract. If None, loads
   173→            from probe cache via model_name.
   174→        model_name: Canonical model name for cache lookup (required when
   175→            layer_indices is None).
   176→        max_length: Max token sequence length.
   177→
   178→    Returns:
   179→        Tuple of (features, attention_mask):
   180→            features shape: (1, num_layers, seq_len, hidden_dim)
   181→            attention_mask shape: (1, seq_len)
   182→
   183→    Raises:
   184→        RuntimeError: If layer_indices is None and no probe cache exists for model_name.
   185→    """
   186→    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
   187→
   188→    if layer_indices is None:
   189→        if model_name is None:
   190→            msg = (
   191→                "layer_indices is None and model_name is None — "
   192→                "cannot load from probe cache without a model name."
   193→            )
   194→            raise RuntimeError(msg)
   195→        cache = load_probe_cache(model_name)
   196→        if cache is None:
   197→            msg = (
   198→                f"layer_indices is None but no probe cache found for '{model_name}'. "
   199→                "Run probe_model() and save_probe_cache() first."
   200→            )
   201→            raise RuntimeError(msg)
   202→        layer_indices = cache["attention_layer_indices"]
   203→

  <name>Task 1: D2LTrainConfig + _compute_kl_ce_loss + 7 unit tests</name>
  <files>libs/model-training/src/model_training/d2l_train.py, libs/model-training/tests/test_d2l_train.py</files>
  <behavior>
    - test_d2l_train_config_pydantic_validation: D2LTrainConfig rejects negative lr, negative alpha; accepts valid defaults
    - test_d2l_train_config_model_dump: config.model_dump() returns dict with all fields, JSON-serializable via json.dumps
    - test_kl_zero_when_equal: _compute_kl_ce_loss with identical student/teacher logits returns KL < 1e-5
    - test_kl_positive_when_different: _compute_kl_ce_loss with different logits returns KL > 0
    - test_ce_loss_valid_shape: CE loss on answer span produces finite scalar tensor
    - test_blended_loss_respects_alpha: alpha=1.0 gives total==KL, alpha=0.0 gives total==CE (within tolerance)
    - test_answer_start_masking: With answer_start=3 on seq_len=5, loss only considers last 2 positions (verify by comparing loss with full-sequence vs masked)
  </behavior>
  <action>
    Create `libs/model-training/src/model_training/d2l_train.py` with:

    1. `D2LTrainConfig(BaseModel)` Pydantic model per CONTEXT.md spec:
       - Fields: base_model_name (str, default "Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-Next"), sakana_checkpoint_path (str), num_steps (int, 100), lr (float, 2e-4), alpha (float, 0.5), temperature (float, 2.0), checkpoint_every (int, 100), full_checkpoint_every (int, 500), checkpoint_dir (str, "./checkpoints"), experiment_name (str, "d2l-qwen3"), dry_run (bool, False), smoke_test (bool, False), dataset_path (str | None, None), grad_clip (float, 1.0), warmup_steps (int, 10), lora_r (int, 8), max_length (int, 512)
       - Add Pydantic field validators: lr > 0, 0 <= alpha <= 1, temperature > 0, num_steps > 0
       - Use `from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator`

    2. `_compute_kl_ce_loss(student_logits, teacher_logits, answer_start, config)` pure function:
       - Signature: `(student_logits: torch.Tensor, teacher_logits: torch.Tensor, answer_start: int, config: D2LTrainConfig) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, dict[str, float]]`
       - Slice both logits to `[:, answer_start:, :]` for answer span masking
       - KL: `F.kl_div(F.log_softmax(s/T, dim=-1), F.softmax(t/T, dim=-1), reduction="batchmean")` — MUST use reduction="batchmean"
       - CE: `F.cross_entropy(s.reshape(-1, vocab), t.argmax(-1).reshape(-1))`
       - Blend: `total = alpha * kl + (1 - alpha) * ce`
       - Return `(total_loss, {"kl_loss": kl.item(), "ce_loss": ce.item(), "total_loss": total.item()})`

    3. Module boilerplate: `from __future__ import annotations`, `__all__ = ["train_d2l_qwen3", "D2LTrainConfig"]`, logger, Google-style docstrings
    4. Stub `def train_d2l_qwen3(config: D2LTrainConfig) -> dict[str, Any]: ...` with `raise NotImplementedError("Implemented in Plan 02")` — needed for __init__.py export to resolve

    Create `libs/model-training/tests/test_d2l_train.py` with the 7 tests listed in behavior. Tests import only `D2LTrainConfig` and `_compute_kl_ce_loss` from `model_training.d2l_train`. Tests use `import torch` directly (torch is available in test env). No GPU mocking needed — these are pure tensor/pydantic tests.

    For Pydantic validation tests, use `pytest.raises(ValidationError)` for invalid values.
  </action>

  </verify>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 1: Training loop, checkpoint, MLflow, modes, CLI</name>
  <files>libs/model-training/src/model_training/d2l_train.py</files>
  <action>
    Extend d2l_train.py (which already has D2LTrainConfig and _compute_kl_ce_loss from Plan 01) with the following functions. ALL GPU-dependent imports (torch, transformers, mlflow, etc.) MUST be deferred inside function bodies with `# noqa: PLC0415` per project pattern (INFRA-05).

    1. **`_training_step(record, base_model, tokenizer, hypernet, hc, config) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, dict[str, float]]`**
       Two-pass teacher/student separation (TRAIN-02):
       - Pass 1: `features, attn_mask = extract_activations_with_model(text=record["activation_text"], model=base_model, tokenizer=tokenizer, layer_indices=list(hc.layer_indices), max_length=config.max_length)` — context only, NO answer tokens
       - Hypernetwork forward (OUTSIDE torch.no_grad): `lora_dict, _ = hypernet.generate_weights(features, attn_mask, None)`
       - Pass 2 teacher: tokenize record["teacher_text"], run base_model with `torch.no_grad()`, get teacher_logits
       - Pass 2 student: compute answer_start = len(tokenizer(record["activation_text"], truncation=True, max_length=config.max_length)["input_ids"]), run base_model inside `apply_functional_lora(base_model, lora_dict, hc)` context with same teacher_inputs, get student_logits
       - Return `_compute_kl_ce_loss(student_logits, teacher_logits, answer_start, config)`
       - Keep base_model.eval() throughout. Hypernet stays in train() mode.

    2. **`_save_checkpoint(step, hypernet, optimizer, scheduler, config, hc, best_loss, full=False) -> Path`** (TRAIN-05)
       - Tiered: lightweight (hypernet state_dict, config.model_dump() as config_json, step, attention_layer_indices from hc, best_loss) every checkpoint_every steps
       - Full: adds optimizer_state_dict, scheduler_state_dict, torch.get_rng_state(), cuda rng if available — every full_checkpoint_every steps
       - Format: `torch.save(payload, ckpt_dir / f"ckpt-{step}.pt")`
       - Return the checkpoint path

    3. **`_setup_mlflow(config: D2LTrainConfig) -> None`**
       - Set tracking URI from `MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI` env var or default "./mlruns"
       - Set experiment name from config.experiment_name

    4. **`train_d2l_qwen3(config: D2LTrainConfig) -> dict[str, Any]`** — REPLACE the NotImplementedError stub from Plan 01
       - Mode dispatch: if config.dry_run → call `_dry_run_validate_shapes(config)` and return; if config.smoke_test → set config.num_steps = min(config.num_steps, 5)
       - Setup: deferred imports of torch, transformers, mlflow inside function body
       - Load tokenizer: `AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(config.base_model_name)`
       - Load base model: `AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(config.base_model_name, output_hidden_states=True)` → `.eval()`
       - Build hypernet config: `build_qwen3_hypernet_config(aggregator_config=get_aggregator_config(config.sakana_checkpoint_path))`
       - Create hypernet and transfer: `transfer_aggregator_weights(hypernet, config.sakana_checkpoint_path)` → `.train()`
       - Detect device: cuda if available, else cpu
       - Move models to device
       - Optimizer (TRAIN-04): `trainable_params = [p for p in hypernet.parameters() if p.requires_grad]`, `AdamW(trainable_params, lr=config.lr)`
       - Scheduler: `SequentialLR([LinearLR(start_factor=0.01, total_iters=warmup_steps), CosineAnnealingLR(T_max=max(1, num_steps - warmup_steps), eta_min=1e-6)], milestones=[warmup_steps])`
       - Data: for smoke_test/dry_run use `generate_needle_dataset()`, for full training use `load_jsonl(config.dataset_path)` → `split_by_task_id()`
       - MLflow: `_setup_mlflow(config)`, wrap loop in `mlflow.start_run(run_name=...)`, `mlflow.log_params(config.model_dump())`
       - Training loop: iterate records cycling if num_steps > len(records), call `_training_step`, `loss.backward()`, `clip_grad_norm_(trainable_params, config.grad_clip)`, `optimizer.step()`, `scheduler.step()`, `optimizer.zero_grad()`, log metrics via mlflow
       - Checkpoint: if step % checkpoint_every == 0 save lightweight, if step % full_checkpoint_every == 0 save full, log artifact to mlflow
       - Track best_loss, return summary dict with final_loss, best_loss, num_steps_completed, checkpoint_dir
       - For smoke_test: assert all losses finite and loss[-1] < loss[0]

    5. **`_dry_run_validate_shapes(config: D2LTrainConfig) -> dict[str, Any]`** (TRAIN-06)
       - Same setup as train (load model, hypernet, tokenizer)
       - Generate 1 needle record
       - Run single _training_step WITHOUT backward/optimizer
       - Assert features shape, lora_dict shapes, student_logits shape, teacher_logits shape
       - Log all shape validations, return shape summary dict, exit cleanly

    6. **CLI `if __name__ == "__main__":` block**
       - argparse with all D2LTrainConfig fields as CLI flags
       - `--dry-run` and `--smoke-test` as `store_true`
       - `--dataset` maps to dataset_path
       - `D2LTrainConfig(**vars(args))` → `train_d2l_qwen3(config)`

    ANTI-PATTERNS TO AVOID (from RESEARCH.md):
    - Do NOT call hypernet.generate_weights() inside torch.no_grad() — severs autograd
    - Do NOT use reduction='mean' or 'sum' for F.kl_div — MUST use 'batchmean'
    - Do NOT include answer tokens in activation_text pass
    - Do NOT put frozen aggregator params in optimizer
    - Do NOT call scheduler.step() before optimizer.step()
    - Do NOT set base_model to train() mode
  </action>

  </verify>
  <done>train_d2l_qwen3 implemented with two-pass training step, optimizer scoped to trainable params, tiered checkpointing, MLflow tracking, dry-run/smoke-test modes, and CLI entry point. All GPU imports deferred.</done>

  <files>libs/model-training/tests/test_d2l_train.py</files>
  <behavior>
    - test_frozen_aggregator_zero_grad: After backward through a fake hypernet with frozen aggregator, aggregator.* params have None grad
    - test_trainable_head_nonzero_grad: After backward through a fake hypernet, head.* params have non-None grad
    - test_optimizer_scoped_to_trainable: AdamW created with filtered trainable params has state only for head params, not aggregator
    - test_checkpoint_save_load_roundtrip: _save_checkpoint writes a file, torch.load reads it back without errors
    - test_checkpoint_contains_required_keys: Saved checkpoint dict has keys: hypernet_state_dict, config_json, step, attention_layer_indices, best_loss
    - test_two_pass_separation_shapes: Fake two-pass with different activation_text and teacher_text returns correct student/teacher logit shapes
    - test_smoke_test_loss_finite: 5-step loop using _compute_kl_ce_loss with random logits produces finite loss each step
  </behavior>
  <action>
    Add 7 new tests to `libs/model-training/tests/test_d2l_train.py` (which already has 7 tests from Plan 01).

    Test strategy (matching project pattern from test_d2l_lora.py, test_d2l_weight_transfer.py):
    - Use `sys.modules` injection for GPU deps where needed (transformers, mlflow)
    - Use `torch.nn.Module` subclasses as fake models (not SimpleNamespace for nn.Module behavior)
    - All tests are CPU-only, no real Qwen3 model

    Specific test implementations:

    **test_frozen_aggregator_zero_grad:**
    - Create a small nn.Module with `self.aggregator = nn.Linear(8, 8)` and `self.head = nn.Linear(8, 4)`
    - Freeze aggregator: `p.requires_grad_(False)` for aggregator params
    - Forward through head only, compute loss, backward
    - Assert aggregator params have `p.grad is None`, head params have `p.grad is not None`

    **test_trainable_head_nonzero_grad:**
    - Same setup as above, verify head.weight.grad and head.bias.grad are not None after backward

    **test_optimizer_scoped_to_trainable:**
    - Create fake module, freeze aggregator, filter trainable params
    - Create AdamW with trainable_params only
    - Verify `len(optimizer.param_groups[0]['params'])` equals number of head parameters (not total)

    **test_checkpoint_save_load_roundtrip:**
    - Import `_save_checkpoint` from d2l_train
    - Create fake hypernet (nn.Module), fake optimizer, fake scheduler (can use MagicMock for state_dict)
    - Create D2LTrainConfig with tmp_path as checkpoint_dir
    - Call _save_checkpoint, then torch.load the result with weights_only=False
    - Assert no errors and loaded dict has expected keys

    **test_checkpoint_contains_required_keys:**
    - Same as above but explicitly check: "hypernet_state_dict", "config_json", "step", "attention_layer_indices", "best_loss" are all in loaded dict
    - For full=True checkpoint, additionally check: "optimizer_state_dict", "scheduler_state_dict", "rng_state"

    **test_two_pass_separation_shapes:**
    - Create random tensors simulating student_logits and teacher_logits with shape (1, 10, 50) (batch=1, seq=10, vocab=50)
    - Verify shapes match and _compute_kl_ce_loss accepts them with answer_start=5

    **test_smoke_test_loss_finite:**
    - Loop 5 times: create random student/teacher logits, call _compute_kl_ce_loss with config, assert loss.isfinite()
    - Verify all 5 losses are finite floats

    Use `import torch` directly (available in test env). Use `pytest` fixtures where appropriate (tmp_path for checkpoint tests).
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>uv run pytest libs/model-training/tests/test_d2l_train.py -x -v 2>&1 | tail -30</automated>
  </verify>
  <done>14 total tests pass (7 from Plan 01 + 7 new). Frozen params verified, checkpoint roundtrip works, two-pass shapes correct, smoke-test losses finite.</done>

### v7.0 Hypernetwork Training (Planned)

**Milestone Goal:** Build the KL-divergence context distillation training pipeline for HyperLoRA on Qwen3-Coder-Next, enabling the hypernetwork to compress procedural coding knowledge from trajectories into LoRA adapter weights.

- [x] **Phase 25: Configuration & Data Pipeline** — Config helpers and full data pipeline with zero GPU dependencies (completed 2026-03-13)
- [x] **Phase 26: Architecture Probe & Activation Extraction** — Dynamic attention layer discovery and pre-loaded model activation extraction (completed 2026-03-13)
- [x] **Phase 27: Partial Weight Transfer** — Freeze aggregator, reinitialize head for Qwen3-Coder-Next output dimensions (completed 2026-03-13)
- [x] **Phase 28: Functional LoRA Injection** — Autograd-safe context manager for hypernetwork training (completed 2026-03-16)
- [ ] **Phase 29: Training Loop Integration** — KL+CE distillation training script with dry-run and smoke-test modes

## Phase Details

### Phase 25: Configuration & Data Pipeline
**Goal**: The config helpers and data pipeline are fully operational with all 5 data tests passing on CPU in CI, providing the config dict and dataset functions every downstream phase depends on.
**Depends on**: Nothing (first phase of v7.0)
**Requirements**: CFG-01, CFG-02, DATA-01, DATA-02, DATA-03, DATA-04, DATA-05, DATA-06, TEST-02
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. `get_d2l_qwen3_config()` returns a dict with correct Qwen3-Coder-Next dimensions (hidden_size, attention layer indices, GQA heads) without loading model weights
  2. `build_qwen3_hypernet_config()` constructs a HypernetConfig with exactly 12 attention layer indices discovered dynamically from config
  3. `format_for_distillation()` produces records with separate `activation_text` (no answer tokens) and `teacher_text` (with answer) — verifiable by inspecting output fields
  4. JSONL round-trip: a dataset written with `save_jsonl()` loads back with `load_jsonl()` byte-for-byte identical
  5. Train/test split has zero task-ID overlap: `set(train_ids) & set(test_ids) == set()` after the full pipeline runs
**Plans:** 2/2 plans complete
Plans:
- [x] 25-01-PLAN.md — Config helpers + core data pipeline (JSONL, needle, split, distillation format) with tests
- [ ] 25-02-PLAN.md — Trajectory generation from HumanEval, LLM augmentation, GitHub mining stubs

### Phase 26: Architecture Probe & Activation Extraction
**Goal**: The architecture probe correctly identifies exactly 12 standard attention layers in Qwen3-Coder-Next (not DeltaNet), and activation extraction accepts a pre-loaded model reference rather than loading weights per call.
**Depends on**: Phase 25
**Requirements**: ARCH-01, ARCH-02
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. `build_qwen3_hypernet_config()` discovers exactly 12 attention layer indices (`[3, 7, 11, ..., 47]`) from `config.layer_types` without loading 80GB of weights
  2. `extract_activations_with_model()` accepts a pre-loaded model and returns hidden states only from the specified attention layer indices (not from DeltaNet layers)
  3. Tests using `sys.modules` injection pass on CPU in CI with no GPU installed
**Plans:** 1/1 plans complete
Plans:
- [x] 26-01-PLAN.md — Probe module (probe_model, cache, extract_activations_with_model) + config/extraction wiring

### Phase 27: Partial Weight Transfer
**Goal**: Hypernetwork aggregator weights transfer from the `qwen_4b_d2l` checkpoint with aggregator parameters frozen and head re-initialized for Qwen3-Coder-Next's output dimensions, verified by key-presence assertions.
**Depends on**: Phase 26
**Requirements**: XFER-01, XFER-02
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. After loading, all `aggregator.*` parameters have `requires_grad=False` — confirmed by iterating named parameters
  2. After loading, `head.*` parameters are absent from the checkpoint (in `missing_keys`) and have the correct output shape for 12 Qwen3-Coder-Next attention layers
  3. A post-load assertion explicitly fails if any expected aggregator key is missing from the loaded state dict
**Plans:** 1/1 plans complete
Plans:
- [ ] 27-01-PLAN.md — Weight transfer functions (transfer_aggregator_weights, get_aggregator_config, assertions) + tests

### Phase 28: Functional LoRA Injection
**Goal**: The `apply_functional_lora` context manager patches target modules using `F.linear` with live hypernetwork tensor graph nodes, preserving autograd continuity through the hypernetwork, and restores original forward methods on exit.
**Depends on**: Phase 27
**Requirements**: LORA-01, LORA-02, LORA-03
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. Generated LoRA tensors have non-None `.grad` after `loss.backward()` — hypernetwork parameters receive gradient signal
  2. All hypernetwork parameters have non-None `.grad` after a backward pass through the functional LoRA path
  3. After exiting the context manager, target module forward methods are restored to their originals with no side effects (verified by calling the module again outside the context)
**Plans:** 1/1 plans complete
Plans:
- [x] 28-01-PLAN.md — apply_functional_lora context manager + autograd/restoration TDD tests

### Phase 29: Training Loop Integration
**Goal**: The complete KL-divergence context distillation training script assembles all prior components, with dry-run mode verifying shapes on CPU in under 30 seconds and smoke-test mode confirming finite, decreasing loss over 5 real training steps.
**Depends on**: Phase 28
**Requirements**: TRAIN-01, TRAIN-02, TRAIN-03, TRAIN-04, TRAIN-05, TRAIN-06, TRAIN-07, TEST-01
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. `--dry-run` completes in under 30 seconds on CPU, validates all tensor shapes in one forward pass, and exits 0 with no optimizer step taken
  2. `--smoke-test` runs 5 training steps, reports finite loss at every step, and step-5 loss is lower than step-1 loss
  3. The KL loss unit test returns zero when `student_logits == teacher_logits` (formula correctness verified)
  4. Optimizer is scoped exclusively to trainable parameters (head + projections only) — aggregator parameters have zero gradient after backward
  5. Checkpoint files written every N steps contain model state, config, step count, and attention layer indices — readable back without errors
**Plans:** 2 plans
Plans:
- [ ] 29-01-PLAN.md — D2LTrainConfig + _compute_kl_ce_loss + 7 unit tests + mlflow dep + exports
- [ ] 29-02-PLAN.md — Full training loop (train_d2l_qwen3, checkpoint, MLflow, dry-run, smoke-test, CLI) + 7 remaining tests

   163→  <action>
   164→    Extend d2l_train.py (which already has D2LTrainConfig and _compute_kl_ce_loss from Plan 01) with the following functions. ALL GPU-dependent imports (torch, transformers, mlflow, etc.) MUST be deferred inside function bodies with `# noqa: PLC0415` per project pattern (INFRA-05).
   165→
   166→    1. **`_training_step(record, base_model, tokenizer, hypernet, hc, config) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, dict[str, float]]`**
   167→       Two-pass teacher/student separation (TRAIN-02):
   168→       - Pass 1: `features, attn_mask = extract_activations_with_model(text=record["activation_text"], model=base_model, tokenizer=tokenizer, layer_indices=list(hc.layer_indices), max_length=config.max_length)` — context only, NO answer tokens
   169→       - Hypernetwork forward (OUTSIDE torch.no_grad): `lora_dict, _ = hypernet.generate_weights(features, attn_mask, None)`
   170→       - Pass 2 teacher: tokenize record["teacher_text"], run base_model with `torch.no_grad()`, get teacher_logits
   171→       - Pass 2 student: compute answer_start = len(tokenizer(record["activation_text"], truncation=True, max_length=config.max_length)["input_ids"]), run base_model inside `apply_functional_lora(base_model, lora_dict, hc)` context with same teacher_inputs, get student_logits
   172→       - Return `_compute_kl_ce_loss(student_logits, teacher_logits, answer_start, config)`
   173→       - Keep base_model.eval() throughout. Hypernet stays in train() mode.
   174→
   175→    2. **`_save_checkpoint(step, hypernet, optimizer, scheduler, config, hc, best_loss, full=False) -> Path`** (TRAIN-05)
   176→       - Tiered: lightweight (hypernet state_dict, config.model_dump() as config_json, step, attention_layer_indices from hc, best_loss) every checkpoint_every steps
   177→       - Full: adds optimizer_state_dict, scheduler_state_dict, torch.get_rng_state(), cuda rng if available — every full_checkpoint_every steps
   178→       - Format: `torch.save(payload, ckpt_dir / f"ckpt-{step}.pt")`
   179→       - Return the checkpoint path
   180→
   181→    3. **`_setup_mlflow(config: D2LTrainConfig) -> None`**
   182→       - Set tracking URI from `MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI` env var or default "./mlruns"
   183→       - Set experiment name from config.experiment_name
   184→
   185→    4. **`train_d2l_qwen3(config: D2LTrainConfig) -> dict[str, Any]`** — REPLACE the NotImplementedError stub from Plan 01
   186→       - Mode dispatch: if config.dry_run → call `_dry_run_validate_shapes(config)` and return; if config.smoke_test → set config.num_steps = min(config.num_steps, 5)
   187→       - Setup: deferred imports of torch, transformers, mlflow inside function body
   188→       - Load tokenizer: `AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(config.base_model_name)`
   189→       - Load base model: `AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(config.base_model_name, output_hidden_states=True)` → `.eval()`
   190→       - Build hypernet config: `build_qwen3_hypernet_config(aggregator_config=get_aggregator_config(config.sakana_checkpoint_path))`
   191→       - Create hypernet and transfer: `transfer_aggregator_weights(hypernet, config.sakana_checkpoint_path)` → `.train()`
   192→       - Detect device: cuda if available, else cpu

   194→       - Optimizer (TRAIN-04): `trainable_params = [p for p in hypernet.parameters() if p.requires_grad]`, `AdamW(trainable_params, lr=config.lr)`
   195→       - Scheduler: `SequentialLR([LinearLR(start_factor=0.01, total_iters=warmup_steps), CosineAnnealingLR(T_max=max(1, num_steps - warmup_steps), eta_min=1e-6)], milestones=[warmup_steps])`
   196→       - Data: for smoke_test/dry_run use `generate_needle_dataset()`, for full training use `load_jsonl(config.dataset_path)` → `split_by_task_id()`
   197→       - MLflow: `_setup_mlflow(config)`, wrap loop in `mlflow.start_run(run_name=...)`, `mlflow.log_params(config.model_dump())`
   198→       - Training loop: iterate records cycling if num_steps > len(records), call `_training_step`, `loss.backward()`, `clip_grad_norm_(trainable_params, config.grad_clip)`, `optimizer.step()`, `scheduler.step()`, `optimizer.zero_grad()`, log metrics via mlflow
   199→       - Checkpoint: if step % checkpoint_every == 0 save lightweight, if step % full_checkpoint_every == 0 save full, log artifact to mlflow

   120→### Phase 28: Functional LoRA Injection
   121→**Goal**: The `apply_functional_lora` context manager patches target modules using `F.linear` with live hypernetwork tensor graph nodes, preserving autograd continuity through the hypernetwork, and restores original forward methods on exit.
   122→**Depends on**: Phase 27
   123→**Requirements**: LORA-01, LORA-02, LORA-03
   124→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   125→  1. Generated LoRA tensors have non-None `.grad` after `loss.backward()` — hypernetwork parameters receive gradient signal
   126→  2. All hypernetwork parameters have non-None `.grad` after a backward pass through the functional LoRA path
   127→  3. After exiting the context manager, target module forward methods are restored to their originals with no side effects (verified by calling the module again outside the context)
   128→**Plans:** 1/1 plans complete

   132→### Phase 29: Training Loop Integration
   133→**Goal**: The complete KL-divergence context distillation training script assembles all prior components, with dry-run mode verifying shapes on CPU in under 30 seconds and smoke-test mode confirming finite, decreasing loss over 5 real training steps.
   134→**Depends on**: Phase 28
   135→**Requirements**: TRAIN-01, TRAIN-02, TRAIN-03, TRAIN-04, TRAIN-05, TRAIN-06, TRAIN-07, TEST-01
   136→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   137→  1. `--dry-run` completes in under 30 seconds on CPU, validates all tensor shapes in one forward pass, and exits 0 with no optimizer step taken
   138→  2. `--smoke-test` runs 5 training steps, reports finite loss at every step, and step-5 loss is lower than step-1 loss
   139→  3. The KL loss unit test returns zero when `student_logits == teacher_logits` (formula correctness verified)
   140→  4. Optimizer is scoped exclusively to trainable parameters (head + projections only) — aggregator parameters have zero gradient after backward
   141→  5. Checkpoint files written every N steps contain model state, config, step count, and attention layer indices — readable back without errors

   145→- [ ] 29-02-PLAN.md — Full training loop (train_d2l_qwen3, checkpoint, MLflow, dry-run, smoke-test, CLI) + 7 remaining tests

1→# Requirements: Rune v7.0 Hypernetwork Training
     2→
     3→**Defined:** 2026-03-13
     4→**Core Value:** A local coding agent that learns from its own coding trajectories, building persistent parametric memory that scales independently of context window size.
     5→
     6→## v7.0 Requirements
     7→
     8→Requirements for KL-divergence context distillation training pipeline. Each maps to roadmap phases.
     9→
    10→### Configuration
    11→
    12→- [ ] **CFG-01**: `get_d2l_qwen3_config()` returns Qwen3-Coder-Next dimensions (hidden_size, attention layer indices, GQA heads) via AutoConfig
    13→- [ ] **CFG-02**: `build_qwen3_hypernet_config()` constructs HypernetConfig targeting Qwen3-Coder-Next with dynamic layer discovery via `config.layer_types`
    14→
    15→### Data Pipeline
    16→
    17→- [ ] **DATA-01**: `format_for_distillation()` converts trajectory JSON to `{trajectory, query, answer}` JSONL records with full procedural history
    18→- [ ] **DATA-02**: `generate_needle_dataset()` creates needle-in-haystack synthetic records for smoke testing context distillation
    19→- [ ] **DATA-03**: `save_jsonl()` and `load_jsonl()` provide round-trip JSONL persistence
    20→- [x] **DATA-04**: `generate_trajectory_dataset()` generates coding trajectories from benchmark datasets (HumanEval, MBPP, etc.)
    21→- [x] **DATA-05**: `augment_trajectories()` produces 3-5 augmented variants per validated trajectory
    22→- [ ] **DATA-06**: Train/test split enforced at task-ID level before augmentation, with no task-ID crossing the boundary
    23→
    24→### Architecture Probe
    25→
    26→- [x] **ARCH-01**: Dynamic attention layer discovery identifies exactly the standard attention layers (not DeltaNet) from hybrid model config
    27→- [x] **ARCH-02**: `extract_activations_with_model()` extracts hidden states from pre-loaded model at specified attention layer indices only
    28→
    29→### Weight Transfer
    30→
    31→- [x] **XFER-01**: Partial weight transfer from `qwen_4b_d2l` checkpoint freezes `aggregator.*` parameters (`requires_grad=False`)
    32→- [x] **XFER-02**: Head (`head.*`) is re-initialized with correct output dimensions for Qwen3-Coder-Next's 12 attention layers
    33→
    34→### Functional LoRA
    35→
    36→- [x] **LORA-01**: `apply_functional_lora()` context manager patches target modules with `F.linear` preserving autograd graph
    37→- [x] **LORA-02**: Generated LoRA tensors have non-None `.grad` after backward pass (autograd continuity verified)
    38→- [x] **LORA-03**: Original forward methods restored on context manager exit with no side effects
    39→
    40→### Training Loop
    41→
    42→- [ ] **TRAIN-01**: KL-divergence context distillation training script with CLI arguments matching specification
    43→- [ ] **TRAIN-02**: Two-pass teacher/student separation: activations from `[trajectory+query]` only, teacher logits from `[trajectory+query+answer]`
    44→- [ ] **TRAIN-03**: Blended loss `α*KL + (1-α)*CE` with configurable temperature and blending coefficient
    45→- [ ] **TRAIN-04**: AdamW optimizer scoped to trainable parameters only (head + projections), with cosine LR schedule and warmup
    46→- [ ] **TRAIN-05**: Checkpoint saving every N steps with model state, config, step count, and attention layer indices
    47→- [ ] **TRAIN-06**: Dry-run mode (`--dry-run`) validates all tensor shapes in one forward pass, exits 0 on success
    48→- [ ] **TRAIN-07**: Smoke-test mode (`--smoke-test`) runs 5 training steps, verifies finite loss and non-None gradients
    49→
    50→### Testing
    51→
    52→- [ ] **TEST-01**: 14 training tests covering shape verification, gradient flow, frozen/trainable params, functional LoRA, KL loss, two-pass separation
    53→- [ ] **TEST-02**: 5 data pipeline tests covering needle format, JSONL round-trip, augmentation count, task-ID split integrity
    54→
    55→## v7.x Requirements
    56→
    57→Deferred to after baseline pipeline is validated working.
    58→
    59→### Observability
    60→
    61→- **OBS-01**: TensorBoard/wandb integration for loss curve visualization
    62→- **OBS-02**: Per-step structured logging (kl_loss, ce_loss, total_loss, grad_norm, gpu_mem_gb)
    63→
    64→### Advanced Data
    65→
    66→- **ADATA-01**: LLM-assisted needle generation via Ollama for improved diversity
    67→- **ADATA-02**: Multi-document batched distillation with attention masking
    68→
    69→## Out of Scope
    70→
    71→| Feature | Reason |
    72→|---------|--------|
    73→| MSE loss on LoRA weights | Weight space permutation symmetry makes L2 distance mathematically unsound |
    74→| PEFT `get_peft_model` during training | Severs autograd graph; silent failure mode where hypernetwork never updates |
    75→| Including DeltaNet layers in activation extraction | Different module structure, incompatible with Perceiver trained on standard attention |
    76→| Per-document gradient-based context distillation | Defeats purpose of amortized hypernetwork; minutes per document vs <1s |
    77→| Concurrent training + inference | CUDA OOM confirmed; sequential scheduling required |
    78→| Modifying `DocToLoraHypernetwork` in `hypernetwork.py` | Hash-based path unchanged; all work on Sakana HyperLoRA perceiver |
    79→| Augmentation before train/test split | Inflates evaluation metrics via task-family leakage |
    80→

1→# Phase 29: Training Loop Integration - Context
     2→
     3→**Gathered:** 2026-03-16
     4→**Status:** Ready for planning
     5→
     6→<domain>
     7→## Phase Boundary
     8→
     9→Complete KL-divergence context distillation training script assembling all Phase 25-28 components (config, data pipeline, activation extraction, weight transfer, functional LoRA injection) into `d2l_train.py`. Dry-run mode validates shapes on GPU. Smoke-test mode confirms finite, decreasing loss over 5 steps. Full mode trains from JSONL dataset with tiered checkpointing and MLflow experiment tracking.
    10→
    11→</domain>
    12→
    13→<decisions>
    14→## Implementation Decisions
    15→
    16→### Script Design & Module Placement
    17→- New file: `libs/model-training/src/model_training/d2l_train.py` — follows `d2l_*` naming pattern
    18→- Invoked via `uv run python -m model_training.d2l_train --dry-run`
    19→- Core function: `train_d2l_qwen3(config: D2LTrainConfig) -> dict[str, Any]`
    20→- `D2LTrainConfig` is a **Pydantic BaseModel** — enables validation, JSON serialization (for checkpoint saving), and `.model_dump()` for logging
    21→- CLI wrapper via `if __name__ == "__main__":` with argparse → `D2LTrainConfig(**vars(args))`
    22→- Export `train_d2l_qwen3` and `D2LTrainConfig` from `__init__.py`
    23→
    24→### Mode Invocation
    25→- `--dry-run` and `--smoke-test` as boolean flags. No flag = full training
    26→- `--dry-run`: **requires GPU**, loads real base model + hypernet, runs one forward pass, validates all tensor shapes, exits 0
    27→- `--smoke-test`: runs 5 training steps (configurable via `--num-steps`), verifies finite loss and decreasing trend
    28→- Full training: `--dataset path/to/train.jsonl` required, `--num-steps` for total steps
    29→
    30→### Loss Computation
    31→- **Blended loss**: `total_loss = alpha * KL + (1 - alpha) * CE`
    32→- Default `alpha=0.5`, configurable via `--alpha` CLI flag
    33→- **KL divergence**: temperature-scaled softmax, default `T=2.0`, configurable via `--temperature`
    34→- **CE target**: teacher hard labels (`argmax(teacher_logits)`) — self-contained distillation
    35→- **Loss masking**: answer tokens only — `activation_text` is a strict prefix of `teacher_text`, `answer_start = len(tokenize(activation_text))`, loss mask zeros for context and ones for answer portion
    36→- Both KL and CE masked to answer span
    37→
    38→### Training Data Flow
    39→- **Dry-run and smoke-test**: `generate_needle_dataset()` — deterministic, no external deps
    40→- **Full training**: `load_jsonl(args.dataset_path)` → `split_by_task_id()` → train/test records
    41→- **Tokenization**: internal — training function loads tokenizer from base model name, tokenizes `activation_text` and `teacher_text` per step
    42→- **Answer boundary**: string prefix match — tokenize both texts, `answer_start = len(act_tokens)`
    43→- **Batching**: Claude's discretion — existing APIs assume batch=1, gradient accumulation if needed
    44→
    45→### Checkpoints
    46→- **Tiered checkpointing**:
    47→  - Every 100 steps (`--checkpoint-every`): lightweight — hypernet `state_dict`, `D2LTrainConfig` as JSON, step count, attention layer indices, best loss
    48→  - Every 500 steps (`--full-checkpoint-every`): full state — adds optimizer state_dict, LR scheduler state, RNG states
    49→- Output directory: `--checkpoint-dir` (default: `./checkpoints`)
    50→- Format: `torch.save()` to `ckpt-{step}.pt`
    51→
    52→### Experiment Tracking
    53→- **MLflow as required dependency** in model-training package
    54→- Supports both local file-based tracking (`./mlruns`) and served MLflow instance (via `MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI` env var)
    55→- Log: experiment params from `config.model_dump()`, per-step metrics (kl_loss, ce_loss, total_loss, grad_norm, lr), checkpoint artifacts
    56→- Experiment name: `d2l-qwen3` (or configurable)
    57→
    58→### Optimizer
    59→- AdamW scoped to trainable parameters only (head + projections, NOT frozen aggregator)
    60→- Cosine LR schedule with warmup
    61→- Default lr=2e-4, configurable via `--lr`
    62→
    63→### Claude's Discretion
    64→- Batching strategy (batch=1 with grad accumulation vs mini-batches)
    65→- Warmup ratio and scheduler details
    66→- Gradient clipping value
    67→- MLflow run naming convention
    68→- Exact return dict structure from `train_d2l_qwen3()`
    69→- How to handle `--resume-from` checkpoint loading (if included)
    70→- Test mock strategy for training loop tests without GPU
    71→
    72→</decisions>
    73→
    74→<specifics>
    75→## Specific Ideas
    76→
    77→- Dry-run requiring GPU is intentional — validates real model shapes, not toy proxies
    78→- Tiered checkpointing: frequent lightweight saves protect against loss of progress, periodic full saves enable exact resume
    79→- MLflow should work seamlessly with both `mlflow ui` locally and a team tracking server via env var
    80→- Pydantic config enables saving exact training configuration as JSON inside checkpoints for reproducibility
    81→
    82→</specifics>
    83→
    84→<code_context>
    85→## Existing Code Insights
    86→
    87→### Reusable Assets
    88→- `d2l_lora.py:apply_functional_lora()`: Context manager for autograd-safe LoRA injection — core of training step
    89→- `d2l_probe.py:extract_activations_with_model()`: Pre-loaded model activation extraction — returns `(features, attn_mask)` shape `(1, n_layers, seq_len, hidden_dim)`
    90→- `d2l_config.py:build_qwen3_hypernet_config()`: Returns `HypernetConfig` with lora_config, layer_indices, feature_sizes
    91→- `d2l_data.py:generate_needle_dataset()`: Deterministic synthetic records for dry-run/smoke-test
    92→- `d2l_data.py:load_jsonl()`, `split_by_task_id()`: Full dataset loading and task-ID-level splitting
    93→- `sakana_d2l.py:transfer_aggregator_weights()`: Loads checkpoint, freezes aggregator, returns trainable hypernet
    94→- `sakana_d2l.py:get_aggregator_config()`: Populates HypernetConfig.aggregator_config from checkpoint
    95→- `trainer.py:train_qlora()`: Reference pattern — cosine LR, warmup_ratio=0.03, deferred GPU imports
    96→
    97→### Established Patterns
    98→- GPU imports deferred inside function bodies with `# noqa: PLC0415` (INFRA-05)
    99→- `sys.modules` injection for test mocking of GPU deps
   100→- `monkeypatch.setattr(torch, 'load')` not dotted-path for deferred imports
   101→- Google-style docstrings, `__all__` exports, module-level logger
   102→- Pydantic already in project (FastAPI dependency)
   103→
   104→### Integration Points
   105→- New file: `libs/model-training/src/model_training/d2l_train.py`
   106→- New tests: `libs/model-training/tests/test_d2l_train.py`
   107→- Update: `libs/model-training/src/model_training/__init__.py` — export train_d2l_qwen3, D2LTrainConfig
   108→- Update: `libs/model-training/pyproject.toml` — add mlflow dependency
   109→- Consumes all Phase 25-28 modules (d2l_config, d2l_data, d2l_probe, d2l_lora, sakana_d2l)
   110→
   111→</code_context>
   112→
   113→<deferred>
   114→## Deferred Ideas
   115→
   116→- TensorBoard/wandb integration (OBS-01, v7.x) — MLflow covers experiment tracking for now
   117→- Per-step structured logging to JSONL file (OBS-02, v7.x) — MLflow metrics serve this purpose
   118→- `--resume-from` checkpoint loading — useful but not in TRAIN-01 through TRAIN-07 requirements
   119→
   120→</deferred>

    90→
    91→## Summary
    92→
    93→Phase 29 assembles all prior phase components (d2l_config, d2l_data, d2l_probe, d2l_lora, sakana_d2l) into a single training script `d2l_train.py`. The script implements knowledge distillation via KL-divergence between student (hypernetwork-adapted base model) and teacher (unmodified base model on full trajectory+answer text). The central training loop extracts activations from activation_text, runs the hypernetwork to generate LoRA weights, patches the base model via `apply_functional_lora`, then computes blended KL+CE loss on the answer token span only.
    94→
    95→All required libraries are already installed: PyTorch 2.10.0 (verified), MLflow 3.10.0 (already in root pyproject.toml), Pydantic 2.12.5. The only pyproject.toml change needed is adding `mlflow` to `libs/model-training/pyproject.toml` dependencies. The KL formula has been verified to return approximately zero when student_logits equal teacher_logits (verified: ~2.98e-8). Frozen aggregator parameters receive None gradients after backward (verified). The SequentialLR warmup + cosine schedule pattern from trainer.py works correctly with optimizer.step() called before scheduler.step().
    96→
    97→The 14 required tests (TEST-01) must all use sys.modules injection or SimpleNamespace fake models to avoid GPU dependencies — matching the established pattern from test_d2l_lora.py and test_d2l_weight_transfer.py. The most complex tests are the two-pass separation verification and the KL zero test; both are pure-tensor tests that do not require a real model.
    98→
    99→**Primary recommendation:** Build `d2l_train.py` as a single file with: D2LTrainConfig Pydantic model, `_compute_kl_ce_loss()` pure function (testable without GPU), `_training_step()` that calls apply_functional_lora, `train_d2l_qwen3()` main loop, and `_dry_run_validate_shapes()` / `_smoke_test()` mode dispatchers.

   103→## Standard Stack
   104→
   105→### Core
   106→| Library | Version | Purpose | Why Standard |
   107→|---------|---------|---------|--------------|
   108→| torch | 2.10.0 | Training loop, loss computation, optimizer, checkpointing | Already installed; AdamW, SequentialLR, clip_grad_norm_ all present |
   109→| pydantic | 2.12.5 | D2LTrainConfig validation, JSON serialization via .model_dump() | Already a FastAPI dep; used in d2l_data.py indirectly |
   110→| mlflow | 3.10.0 | Experiment tracking, metric logging, artifact saving | Already in root pyproject.toml; start_run/log_metric/log_artifact confirmed |
   111→
   112→### Supporting
   113→| Library | Version | Purpose | When to Use |
   114→|---------|---------|---------|-------------|
   115→| torch.optim.AdamW | 2.10.0 | Optimizer scoped to trainable params | All gradient steps |
   116→| torch.optim.lr_scheduler.SequentialLR | 2.10.0 | Chain LinearLR warmup → CosineAnnealingLR | Cosine with warmup in one scheduler |
   117→| torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_ | 2.10.0 | Gradient clipping before optimizer.step() | Every training step; prevents exploding grads |
   118→| torch.nn.functional.kl_div | 2.10.0 | KL divergence loss on log-probs vs probs | TRAIN-03 loss computation |
   119→| torch.nn.functional.cross_entropy | 2.10.0 | CE loss on student logits vs teacher argmax labels | TRAIN-03 blended loss |
   120→
   121→### Alternatives Considered
   122→| Instead of | Could Use | Tradeoff |
   123→|------------|-----------|----------|
   124→| SequentialLR(LinearLR, CosineAnnealingLR) | transformers.get_cosine_schedule_with_warmup | Both work; native torch avoids extra import; trainer.py uses transformers version but native is simpler for custom loop |
   125→| F.kl_div(log_softmax, softmax) | nn.KLDivLoss | F.kl_div is more explicit; reduction='batchmean' is correct default; nn.KLDivLoss wraps the same |

   135→## Architecture Patterns
   136→
   137→### Recommended Project Structure
   138→```
   139→libs/model-training/src/model_training/
   140→├── d2l_train.py         # Phase 29 — complete training script
   141→├── d2l_config.py        # Phase 25 — HypernetConfig builder
   142→├── d2l_data.py          # Phase 25 — data pipeline

   150→
   151→### Pattern 1: D2LTrainConfig as Pydantic BaseModel
   152→**What:** All training hyperparameters in a validated, serializable config object
   153→**When to use:** Always — enables checkpoint JSON, MLflow param logging, argparse integration
   154→
   155→```python
   156→# Source: CONTEXT.md decision + pydantic 2.x docs
   157→from __future__ import annotations
   158→from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
   159→from pathlib import Path
   160→
   161→class D2LTrainConfig(BaseModel):
   162→    base_model_name: str = "Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-Next"
   163→    sakana_checkpoint_path: str
   164→    num_steps: int = 100
   165→    lr: float = 2e-4
   166→    alpha: float = 0.5          # KL/CE blend weight
   167→    temperature: float = 2.0
   168→    checkpoint_every: int = 100
   169→    full_checkpoint_every: int = 500
   170→    checkpoint_dir: str = "./checkpoints"
   171→    experiment_name: str = "d2l-qwen3"
   172→    dry_run: bool = False
   173→    smoke_test: bool = False
   174→    dataset_path: str | None = None
   175→    grad_clip: float = 1.0
   176→    warmup_steps: int = 10
   177→    lora_r: int = 8
   178→    max_length: int = 512
   179→```
   180→
   181→### Pattern 2: Two-Pass Teacher/Student Forward
   182→**What:** Separate forward passes to prevent answer token leakage into activation extraction
   183→**When to use:** Every training step — this is the core TRAIN-02 requirement
   184→
   185→```python
   186→# Source: REQUIREMENTS.md TRAIN-02 + d2l_probe.py extract_activations_with_model()
   187→import torch
   188→
   189→def _training_step(
   190→    record: dict[str, str],
   191→    base_model: Any,
   192→    tokenizer: Any,
   193→    hypernet: Any,
   194→    hc: Any,
   195→    config: D2LTrainConfig,
   196→) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, dict[str, float]]:
   197→    # Pass 1: activation extraction from context-only text (NO answer tokens)
   198→    features, attn_mask = extract_activations_with_model(
   199→        text=record["activation_text"],
   200→        model=base_model,
   201→        tokenizer=tokenizer,
   202→        layer_indices=list(hc.layer_indices),
   203→        max_length=config.max_length,
   204→    )
   205→
   206→    # Hypernetwork forward: features → LoRA A/B tensors (live graph nodes)
   207→    lora_dict, _ = hypernet.generate_weights(features, attn_mask, None)
   208→
   209→    # Pass 2: teacher logits from full text (context + answer)
   210→    teacher_inputs = tokenizer(
   211→        record["teacher_text"],
   212→        return_tensors="pt",
   213→        truncation=True,
   214→        max_length=config.max_length,
   215→    )
   216→    teacher_inputs = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in teacher_inputs.items()}
   217→
   218→    with torch.no_grad():
   219→        teacher_out = base_model(**teacher_inputs)
   220→    teacher_logits = teacher_out.logits  # (1, seq_len, vocab)
   221→
   222→    # Student pass: same full text, but with LoRA-patched model
   223→    act_tokens = tokenizer(
   224→        record["activation_text"],
   225→        truncation=True,
   226→        max_length=config.max_length,
   227→    )
   228→    answer_start = len(act_tokens["input_ids"])
   229→
   230→    with apply_functional_lora(base_model, lora_dict, hc):
   231→        student_out = base_model(**teacher_inputs)
   232→    student_logits = student_out.logits  # (1, seq_len, vocab)
   233→
   234→    loss, metrics = _compute_kl_ce_loss(
   235→        student_logits, teacher_logits, answer_start, config
   236→    )
   237→    return loss, metrics
   238→```
   239→
   240→### Pattern 3: KL+CE Blended Loss with Answer Span Masking
   241→**What:** Pure-tensor loss computation — testable without a real model
   242→**When to use:** Every training step; also standalone in unit tests
   243→
   244→```python
   245→# Source: verified locally with torch 2.10.0
   246→import torch.nn.functional as F
   247→
   248→def _compute_kl_ce_loss(
   249→    student_logits: torch.Tensor,   # (1, seq_len, vocab)
   250→    teacher_logits: torch.Tensor,   # (1, seq_len, vocab)
   251→    answer_start: int,
   252→    config: D2LTrainConfig,
   253→) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, dict[str, float]]:
   254→    """Compute blended KL+CE loss on answer span only.
   255→
   256→    KL returns ~0 when student_logits == teacher_logits (verified).
   257→    """
   258→    T = config.temperature
   259→    alpha = config.alpha
   260→
   261→    # Slice to answer span only
   262→    s_ans = student_logits[:, answer_start:, :]   # (1, ans_len, vocab)
   263→    t_ans = teacher_logits[:, answer_start:, :]   # (1, ans_len, vocab)
   264→
   265→    # KL divergence: kl_div expects log-probs for input, probs for target
   266→    s_log = F.log_softmax(s_ans / T, dim=-1)
   267→    t_prob = F.softmax(t_ans / T, dim=-1)
   268→    kl_loss = F.kl_div(s_log, t_prob, reduction="batchmean")
   269→
   270→    # CE loss: teacher hard labels
   271→    vocab = s_ans.shape[-1]
   272→    t_labels = t_ans.argmax(dim=-1)   # (1, ans_len)
   273→    ce_loss = F.cross_entropy(
   274→        s_ans.reshape(-1, vocab),
   275→        t_labels.reshape(-1),
   276→    )
   277→
   278→    total_loss = alpha * kl_loss + (1.0 - alpha) * ce_loss
   279→    return total_loss, {
   280→        "kl_loss": kl_loss.item(),
   281→        "ce_loss": ce_loss.item(),
   282→        "total_loss": total_loss.item(),
   283→    }
   284→```
   285→
   286→### Pattern 4: Cosine LR with Linear Warmup (SequentialLR)
   287→**What:** Native PyTorch scheduler chain — no additional imports
   288→**When to use:** Always; warmup prevents early divergence
   289→
   290→```python
   291→# Source: verified with torch.optim.lr_scheduler, torch 2.10.0
   292→# CRITICAL: optimizer.step() BEFORE scheduler.step() (PyTorch 1.1+ requirement)
   293→
   294→trainable_params = [p for p in hypernet.parameters() if p.requires_grad]
   295→optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(trainable_params, lr=config.lr)
   296→
   297→warmup_sched = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(
   298→    optimizer,
   299→    start_factor=0.01,
   300→    end_factor=1.0,
   301→    total_iters=config.warmup_steps,
   302→)
   303→cosine_sched = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(
   304→    optimizer,
   305→    T_max=max(1, config.num_steps - config.warmup_steps),
   306→    eta_min=1e-6,
   307→)
   308→scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.SequentialLR(
   309→    optimizer,
   310→    schedulers=[warmup_sched, cosine_sched],
   311→    milestones=[config.warmup_steps],
   312→)
   313→
   314→# In training loop:
   315→loss.backward()
   316→grad_norm = torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(trainable_params, config.grad_clip)
   317→optimizer.step()
   318→scheduler.step()          # AFTER optimizer.step()
   319→optimizer.zero_grad()
   320→```
   321→
   322→### Pattern 5: Tiered Checkpoint Saving
   323→**What:** Frequent lightweight saves (hypernet only) + periodic full saves (optimizer + RNG)
   324→**When to use:** Every N steps per config.checkpoint_every / config.full_checkpoint_every
   325→
   326→```python
   327→# Source: CONTEXT.md decisions + torch.save verified
   328→import torch, json
   329→from pathlib import Path
   330→
   331→def _save_checkpoint(
   332→    step: int, hypernet: Any, optimizer: Any, scheduler: Any,
   333→    config: D2LTrainConfig, hc: Any, best_loss: float, full: bool = False,
   334→) -> Path:
   335→    ckpt_dir = Path(config.checkpoint_dir)
   336→    ckpt_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   337→    path = ckpt_dir / f"ckpt-{step}.pt"
   338→
   339→    payload: dict[str, Any] = {
   340→        "step": step,
   341→        "hypernet_state_dict": hypernet.state_dict(),
   342→        "config_json": config.model_dump(),    # Pydantic → dict for JSON-safe storage
   343→        "attention_layer_indices": list(hc.layer_indices),
   344→        "best_loss": best_loss,
   345→    }
   346→    if full:
   347→        payload["optimizer_state_dict"] = optimizer.state_dict()
   348→        payload["scheduler_state_dict"] = scheduler.state_dict()
   349→        payload["rng_state"] = torch.get_rng_state()
   350→        if torch.cuda.is_available():
   351→            payload["cuda_rng_state"] = torch.cuda.get_rng_state()
   352→
   353→    torch.save(payload, path)
   354→    return path
   355→```

   362→# Source: mlflow 3.10.0 verified locally
